question: Does an ensemble-disagreement gate concentrate rejection on high-error transitions?
baseline: randomly reject the same number of cases
changedCase: two ensemble sets share a confident bias
checks: disagreement computed before target is read
random baseline has equal rejection count
failure: All members agree on the wrong value outside support, so disagreement alone fails to gate the case.
decision: Use disagreement as one diagnostic alongside support and independent constraints.
boundary: The gate is not calibrated as a probability of failure.
